title: Nearly Complete Charge--Spin Conversion via Strain-Eliminated Fermi Pockets in a $d$-Wave Altermagnet
authors: Wancheng Zhang, Zhenhua Zhang, Rui Xiong, Zhihong Lu
abstract: The room-temperature altermagnet \mathrm{KV_2Se_2O} possesses nearly orthogonal flat Fermi surfaces, which in an idealized $d$-wave limit enable complete spin-channel separation and a theoretical charge-to-spin conversion efficiency (CSE) of 100%. Realistic samples, however, host residual elliptical Fermi pockets that enhance charge conductivity while suppressing spin conductivity, drastically reducing the CSE. Here we show that in-plane equibiaxial tensile strain systematically eliminates these parasitic pockets, restoring the flat-band geometry. Our first-principles calculations reveal that the CSE increases monotonically with strain, reaching a record value of approximately 96% at 4% strain. An effective tight-binding model fitted to the computed band structure accurately captures the evolution of the Fermi surface and confirms that the suppression of the pockets -- governed by reduced next-nearest-neighbor hoppings -- is the dominant mechanism for the strain-enhanced CSE. We further identify an unconventional out-of-plane spin current that emerges under tilted electric fields and achieves a CSE of nearly 55% at optimal orientations, offering a promising pathway for field-free perpendicular magnetization switching. Our work establishes strain engineering as a practical route to approach the ultimate conversion limit in altermagnets and provides a design principle for high-efficiency spintronic devices.
summary: This paper investigates how to maximize the efficiency of converting electric charge to spin current in a specific class of materials called d-wave altermagnets. The researchers found that applying physical strain to the crystal structure can eliminate unwanted electronic pathways (parasitic Fermi pockets) that normally dilute the spin signal. By using strain engineering, they demonstrated that the conversion efficiency can be pushed from much lower values to nearly 96%. This discovery provides a practical design principle for creating highly efficient, field-free spintronic devices.
main_problem: The charge-to-spin conversion efficiency (CSE) in the d-wave altermagnet KV2Se2O is limited by residual elliptical Fermi pockets that act as parasitic conduction channels.
main_result: Applying in-plane equibiaxial tensile strain suppresses these parasitic pockets, restoring a flat-band geometry and increasing the CSE to a record value of approximately 96% at 4% strain.
method: The study uses first-principles density functional theory (DFT) combined with Wannier interpolation and an effective two-orbital tight-binding model to simulate strain effects.
model_or_system: The system is the room-temperature metallic d-wave altermagnet KV2Se2O, characterized by a tetragonal P4/mmm space group and momentum-dependent spin splitting without spin-orbit coupling.
key_observables: Charge-to-spin conversion efficiency (CSE), longitudinal and transverse spin conductivities, and spin-resolved Fermi surface geometry.
important_parameters: In-plane equibiaxial tensile strain (1% to 5%), next-nearest-neighbor hopping parameters, and the broadening parameter (10 meV).
main_assumptions: The tight-binding model assumes a simplified uniform scaling of next-nearest-neighbor hopping to mimic strain, which may not fully capture complex anisotropy renormalization.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows crystal structure and the difference between ideal and realistic spin-current generation; Fig 2 displays orbital-resolved band structures and 3D spin-resolved Fermi surfaces under strain; Fig 3 illustrates the strain and angular dependence of CSE; Fig 4 compares the tight-binding model and DFT results for CSE and conductivities.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the altermagnetic system and the problem of low CSE, presents DFT calculations of strain-induced Fermi surface evolution, validates the mechanism using a tight-binding model, and concludes with the discovery of unconventional out-of-plane spin currents and spintronic applications.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Atomic structure, spin-resolved Fermi surface, and schematic of spin-current generation in KV2Se2O. Red and blue denote opposite spin orientations (up and down, respectively). (a) Crystal structure and spin configuration of KV2Se2O. (b) Spin-resolved Fermi surface in the 𝑘𝑧= 0 plane obtained from first-principles calculations. (c) Idealized spin-current generation. The spin-up and spin-down Fermi surfaces are perfectly orthogonal flat sheets. When an electric field E is applied along a ⟨100⟩equivalent direction, a pure longitudinal spin-polarized current J𝑧 ∥= J↑is generated, quantified by the spin conductivity 𝜎𝑧 ∥. When E is applied along a ⟨110⟩equivalent direction, a pure...

FIG. 2. Band structure and Fermi surface of KV2Se2O under equibiaxial tensile strain. (a) Calculated orbital- and spin-resolved band structures along high-symmetry lines at 4% strain. Red and blue dots represent spin-up and spin-down bands, respectively. The size of the dots scales with the projection weight of the V 𝑑𝑥𝑦, 𝑑𝑦𝑧, 𝑑𝑥𝑧, 𝑑𝑥2−𝑦2, and 𝑑𝑧2 orbitals. (b) Spin-resolved three-dimensional Fermi surfaces at 1%, 2%, 3%, 4%, and 5% strain. (c) Spin texture of the two bands crossing the Fermi level [marked in purple and green in (a)] at 4% strain. The arrows indicate the spin orientation, with red (blue) denoting positive (negative) out-of-plane spin component 𝑆𝑧. The surface color encodes...

FIG. 3. Strain and angular dependence of the charge-to-spin conversion efficiency (CSE) and spin conductivities in KV2Se2O evaluated with a broadening parameter 𝜂= 10 meV. (a) Strain-dependent CSE as a function of 𝜂. (b) Polar plots of the longitudinal (left) and transverse (right) CSE under an in-plane rotating electric field at various strain levels. The fourfold symmetry and the relation CSE⊥(𝜑= 45◦) = −CSE∥(𝜑= 0◦) are direct consequences of the 𝑑-wave altermagnetic order. (c) Full-angle dependence of the transverse spin conductivity 𝜎𝑧 𝑦′𝑥′ at 0% strain, shown as a 3D surface combined with a top-view contour projection. (d) Corresponding out-of-plane spin conductivity 𝜎𝑧 𝑧′𝑥′ displayed...

FIG. 4. Strain-induced evolution of the Fermi surface and transport properties in the effective tight-binding model for KV2Se2O. (a) Spin- resolved Fermi surfaces in the 𝑘𝑧= 0 plane as the NNN hopping parameters are uniformly scaled from 1.00 (pristine) to 0.80 in steps of 0.04. Red and blue contours correspond to spin-up and spin-down bands, respectively. (b) CSE evaluated from the Kubo formula with broadening 𝜂= 10 meV and from DFT results (𝜂= 10 meV). The CSE increases monotonically from ∼82% to ∼90% as the pockets are suppressed. (c) Normalized charge conductivity 𝜎and spin conductivity 𝜎𝑠. Solid and dashed lines represent model and DFT results, respectively.
